hawkish_phrases: labour cost pressures were continuing to strengthen and broaden amid high levels of capacity utilisation and tightening labour markets
underlying inflation was expected to increase over the medium term
mark-ups could not be squeezed indefinitely
significant monetary policy stimulus remained essential
dovish_phrases: incoming data had continued to be weaker than expected
the risks surrounding the euro area growth outlook had moved to the downside
measures of underlying inflation remained muted
the near-term growth momentum was likely to be weaker than previously anticipated
expectations of a first ECB deposit facility rate increase had shifted from September 2019 to April 2020
summary: The ECB held rates unchanged with unanimous agreement, reconfirmed forward guidance expecting rates to remain at present levels at least through summer 2019, but acknowledged weaker-than-expected incoming data and explicitly stated that risks to the growth outlook had moved to the downside, creating a mildly dovish bias despite confidence in medium-term inflation convergence.
